# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

Prevent postgres weak auth.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL Client Authentication Configuration File
# ===================================================
# Production Environment - pg_hba.conf
#
# This file controls: which hosts are allowed to connect, how clients
# are authenticated, which PostgreSQL user names they can use, which
# databases they can access.
#
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# Local connections
# =================
# Database administrative login by Unix domain socket
local   all             postgres                                peer

# Local connections for admin users
local   all             admin                                   scram-sha-256

# Local connections for application users
local   all             app                                     scram-sha-256

# Local connections for readonly users
local   all             readonly                                scram-sha-256

# IPv4 local connections
# ======================
# Localhost connections
host    all             postgres        127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             postgres        ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# Admin role - restricted to specific admin servers
# ==================================================
# Admin server subnet (adjust to your network)
host    all             admin           10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256
host    all             admin           192.168.1.0/24          scram-sha-256

# Application role - application servers only
# ============================================
# Application server subnet (adjust to your network)
host    all             app             10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256
host    all             app             192.168.2.0/24          scram-sha-256

# Specific application servers
host    all             app             10.0.2.10/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             app             10.0.2.11/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             app             10.0.2.12/32            scram-sha-256

# Read-only role - reporting and analytics servers
# =================================================
# Readonly server subnet
host    all             readonly        10.0.3.0/24             scram-sha-256
host    all             readonly        192.168.3.0/24          scram-sha-256

# Specific readonly servers
host    all             readonly        10.0.3.10/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             readonly        10.0.3.11/32            scram-sha-256

# SSL/TLS connections (recommended for production)
# ================================================
# Admin with SSL
hostssl all             admin           10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-ca
hostssl all             admin           192.168.1.0/24          scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-ca

# Application with SSL
hostssl all             app             10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256
hostssl all             app             192.168.2.0/24          scram-sha-256

# Readonly with SSL
hostssl all             readonly        10.0.3.0/24             scram-sha-256
hostssl all             readonly        192.168.3.0/24          scram-sha-256

# Replication connections
# =======================
# Replication user for standby servers
local   replication     replication                             scram-sha-256
host    replication     replication     10.0.4.0/24             scram-sha-256
hostssl replication     replication     10.0.4.0/24             scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-ca

# Monitoring connections
# ======================
# Monitoring tools (e.g., Prometheus, Nagios)
host    postgres        monitoring      10.0.5.0/24             scram-sha-256
hostssl postgres        monitoring      10.0.5.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Backup connections
# ==================
# Backup server
host    all             backup          10.0.6.10/32            scram-sha-256
hostssl all             backup          10.0.6.10/32            scram-sha-256

# DENY ALL OTHER CONNECTIONS
# ===========================
# Reject all other connection attempts
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject
host    all             all             ::/0                    reject
